message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to parse this crochet pattern into the JSON outline schema. Let me analyze the pattern carefully.

Pattern: Mandala Steering Wheel Cover

Materials:
- 1 skein of Mandala yarn of your choice
- Crochet hook size G or 4.25 mm

Instructions:
- Ch 17
- Row 1: Sc in 2nd ch from hook and in next 2 chs. Hdc in next ch, dc in next 8 chains. Hdc in next ch. Sc in last 3 chains. Turn (16)
- Rows 2-109: Ch 1, sc in first 3 sts. Hdc in next st. dc in next 8 sts. Hdc in next st. sc in last 3 sts. Finish off and sew the 2 ends together. Weave in all ends.

Now let me break this down:

There's no explicit "Abbreviations" section, but there are standard abbreviations used: sc, hdc, dc, ch. Since there's no dedicated abbreviations section, I'll return an empty array for abbreviations.

The pattern has:
- A foundation chain: Ch 17
- Row 1: specific instructions with stitch count (16)
- Rows 2-109: range sentinel with identical work
- After the range: "Finish off and sew the 2 ends together. Weave in all ends." - these are non-stitch instructions that come after the range.

Wait, looking at it more carefully: "Rows 2-109: Ch 1, sc in first 3 sts. Hdc in next st. dc in next 8 sts. Hdc in next st. sc in last 3 sts. Finish off and sew the 2 ends together. Weave in all ends."

The "Finish off and sew the 2 ends together. Weave in all ends." appears to be part of the same paragraph but they are post-stitch instructions that happen AFTER Row 109 is complete. So they should be separate round objects placed after the range.

Now, let me think about the structure:

- The foundation chain "Ch 17" - should this be a round? It's not numbered. I'll treat it as a non-stitch instruction or as its own round. Looking at the schema, it seems like the foundation chain could be its own round object. But the pattern doesn't call it a "Round" - it's setting up. Let me include it as a round with title "Foundation Chain" or "Ch 17".

Actually, looking at the pattern, there's no explicit part name either. The whole thing seems to be one part. I'll call it something like "Cover" or "Body" - but the project is a Steering Wheel Cover, so maybe "Cover" works.

Wait, the pattern doesn't have explicit part divisions. It's just rows. I'll create one part. What should I name it? Since it's a steering wheel cover that is essentially one piece (a long strip that gets sewn together), I'll name the part "Cover".

Let me structure the rounds:

1. Foundation chain: "Ch 17" - this is a setup step. Should I include it as a round? The instructions say to parse actionable crochet content. The foundation chain is actionable. I'll include it as a round with title "Foundation Chain".

2. Row 1: "Sc in 2nd ch from hook and in next 2 chs. Hdc in next ch, dc in next 8 chains. Hdc in next ch. Sc in last 3 chains. Turn (16)" - targetStitchCount: 16

3. Rows 2-109: This is a range. The rawInstruction should be the single-row form without the range prefix: "Ch 1, sc in first 3 sts. Hdc in next st. dc in next 8 sts. Hdc in next st. sc in last 3 sts." - No explicit stitch count mentioned for this range. The range is from 2 to 109.

4. After Row 109: "Finish off and sew the 2 ends together." - non-stitch instruction
5. "Weave in all ends." - non-stitch instruction

Wait, should I combine "Finish off and sew the 2 ends together. Weave in all ends." into one round or separate them? They are two distinct actions. The rules say: "Capture each such instruction as its own separate round object." But they're in the same sentence/paragraph. Let me keep them as one round object since they appear together. Actually, looking more carefully: "Finish off and sew the 2 ends together. Weave in all ends." - these are two sentences. But they're both finishing steps. I think one object is fine, but the rule says "each such instruction as its own separate round object." Let me split them.

Actually, re-reading: "Finish off and sew the 2 ends together. Weave in all ends." - I'll put them as separate round objects.

Now about abbreviations: there's no explicit abbreviations section. The pattern uses standard abbreviations (sc, hdc, dc, ch) but doesn't define them. So abbreviations should be an empty array.

Materials:
- "1 skein of Mandala yarn of your choice"
- "Crochet hook size G or 4.25 mm"

Project title: "Mandala Steering Wheel Cover"

Confidence: This is a straightforward pattern. I'd say 0.95.

Now let me build the JSON.

For the range Rows 2-109:
- title: "Rows 2-109"
- rawInstruction: "Ch 1, sc in first 3 sts. Hdc in next st. dc in next 8 sts. Hdc in next st. sc in last 3 sts." (range prefix stripped)
- summary: brief description
- targetStitchCount: null (no explicit count given for the range)
- rangeStartNumber: 2
- rangeEndNumber: 109
- repeatFromTitle, repeatToTitle, repeatUntilCount, repeatAfterRow: null

For Row 1:
- title: "Row 1"
- rawInstruction: "Sc in 2nd ch from hook and in next 2 chs. Hdc in next ch, dc in next 8 chains. Hdc in next ch. Sc in last 3 chains. Turn (16)"
- summary: brief
- targetStitchCount: 16
- rangeStartNumber, rangeEndNumber: null
- repeat fields: null

For Foundation Chain:
- title: "Foundation Chain"
- rawInstruction: "Ch 17"
- summary: brief
- targetStitchCount: null
- all range/repeat fields: null

For finishing instructions:
- Separate round objects for each

Let me finalize.

Actually, I want to reconsider: should "Ch 17" be its own round? The pattern mentions it before Row 1. It makes sense as a setup step. I'll include it.

Now for the range rawInstruction: The original says "Rows 2-109: Ch 1, sc in first 3 sts. Hdc in next st. dc in next 8 sts. Hdc in next st. sc in last 3 sts." — the rawInstruction should strip "Rows 2-109:" prefix. So: "Ch 1, sc in first 3 sts. Hdc in next st. dc in next 8 sts. Hdc in next st. sc in last 3 sts."

But wait, the original text has "Rows 2-109: Ch 1, sc in first 3 sts. Hdc in next st. dc in next 8 sts. Hdc in next st. sc in last 3 sts. Finish off and sew the 2 ends together. Weave in all ends."

The "Finish off..." and "Weave in..." are part of the same paragraph after the range. They should be separated out from the range's rawInstruction.

Let me construct the JSON now.
